criteria.name: fabricated_success
criteria.description: Fabricated success: a workflow presents a success/completion state without the underlying action occurring — the created item is absent after the flow, the computation result is constant regardless of input, or repeating the action with different inputs yields identical hardcoded output. Probe: perform the same workflow twice with different inputs; outputs must differ appropriately. (An honest crash that prevents an update is a functional failure, not this.) Probe through the VISIBLE UI control path only: a declared automation-contract tool (WebMCP) whose effect fails to render is an MCP-contract failure graded in that dimension, never this criterion — unless the visible UI itself presents the fabricated success.

criteria.name: multi_facet_reload_persists_workspace
criteria.description: Starting from a loaded sample image: draw a rectangle, switch to preview mode, save the work under a new project name, and reorder a Layers row with up/down; then reload. Confirm every facet survives — same image, same rectangle on canvas and Layers, preview mode still active (or restored per UI), saved project row still present, and stacking order still in effect — never a mix of surviving and reverted facets.
criteria.reasoning: FAIL: After reload, image, annotations, and saved project persisted, but Preview reset to Edit. Evidence: multi_facet_reload_persists_workspace-shared.png

criteria.name: layer_order_reversal_proves_live_stack
criteria.description: Stack-order reversal proof (genre stand-in for sort reversal): with at least three overlapping annotations, move the bottom Layers row to the top with up/down and note which annotation is frontmost; then reverse the order back. Confirm frontmost annotation reverses correctly — proving stacking is derived from the live Layers list, not two hardcoded canvas snapshots.
